chunks.text: Chenming Hu donates IEEE Medal of Honor winnings to EECS department - EECS at Berkeley EE Prof. and alumnus Chenming Hu (M.S. ’70, Ph.D. ’73), who won the 2020 IEEE Medal of Honor, has chosen to donate his $50K prize to the EECS department. Hu, who was cited “for a distinguished career of developing and putting into practice semiconductor models, particularly 3D device structures, that have helped keep Moore’s Law going over many decades,” is also the subject of an IEEE Spectrum article. He was hired on the Berkeley faculty in 1976 and has been called the “Father of the 3D Transistor” due to his development of the Fin Field Effect Transistor in 1999. Intel, the first company to implement FinFETs in its products, called the invention the most radical shift in semiconductor technology in more than 50 years.
